title: Strategy context brief: PM superpower recovery
context_summary: ProductOS should strengthen strategy and discovery judgment before it expands deeper research automation or broader PM autonomy.

enterprise_goal_links.linkage_summary: The next version should help a buyer understand the product bet, evidence chain, and next recommendation without hidden prompt behavior.
enterprise_goal_links.success_signal: Shorter time from mission intake to a strategy-ready discovery packet.

portfolio_bets.rationale: ProductOS already has governed execution patterns, so the highest-leverage next bet is stronger upstream framing and strategic coherence.
Explicit strategy context compounds future discovery quality without widening release claims.

business_model_outcomes.linkage_summary: Clearer strategy artifacts should make ProductOS easier to explain, evaluate, and trust.
The next version should improve judgment support without claiming broader autonomy than the repo can prove.

strategic_constraints.constraint: Keep the stable line and external claim boundary unchanged.
strategic_constraints.consequence: This slice must stay repo-first, internal-first, and proof-driven.

strategic_constraints.constraint: Prefer explicit contracts over prompt-only strategy behavior.
strategic_constraints.consequence: Strategy logic needs schemas, workflow rules, and reference artifacts.

open_questions: Which strategy signals should be visible by default in the PM-facing decision packet?

evidence_refs.justification: The mission defines the PM-facing problem and business goal for the next slice.
The research shows that trusted operating context matters more than isolated drafting acceleration.
